lede: A look at December 1 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Sebastián Piñera and Justin Chadwick, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Bihar
events.desc: In the Indian state of Bihar, Ranvir Sena attacks the CPI (ML) Party Unity stronghold Lakshmanpur-Bathe, killing 63 lower caste people.

events.title: Ukraine
events.desc: Cold War: Ukrainian voters overwhelmingly approve a referendum for independence from the Soviet Union.

events.title: Papua New Guinea
events.desc: Papua New Guinea gains self-government from Australia.

events.title: Croatian Spring
events.desc: Purge of Croatian Spring leaders starts in Yugoslavia at the meeting of the League of Communists at the Karađorđevo estate.

events.title: Nagaland
events.desc: Nagaland, became the 16th state of India.

events.title: Central African Republic
events.desc: The Central African Republic attains self-rule within the French Union.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: A day after the beginning of the Winter War in Finland, the Cajander III Cabinet resigns and is replaced by the Ryti I Cabinet, while the Finnish Parliament move from Helsinki to Kauhajoki to escape the Soviet airstrikes.

events.title: Sergei Kirov
events.desc: Sergei Kirov is assassinated, paving way for the repressive Great Purge, and Vinnytsia massacre by General Secretary of the Communist Party of the Soviet Union, Joseph Stalin.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: In his second State of the Union Address, President Abraham Lincoln reaffirms the necessity of ending slavery as ordered ten weeks earlier in the Emancipation Proclamation.

events.title: Pedro I of Brazil
events.desc: Pedro I is crowned Emperor of Brazil.

births.name: Sebastián Piñera
births.line: Chilean businessman and politician, 35th President of Chile

births.name: Zhu De
births.line: Chinese general and politician, 1st Vice Chairman of the People's Republic of China

births.name: Katherine LaNasa
births.line: American actress, ballet dancer, and choreographer

births.name: George Maxwell Richards
births.line: Trinidadian politician, 4th President of Trinidad and Tobago

deaths.text: Sandra Day O'Connor, first female U.S. Supreme Court Justice
David Ben-Gurion, Israeli politician, 1st Prime Minister of Israel
Charles de Foucauld, French priest and martyr
Alexander I of Russia, emperor and autocrat of Russia
Pope Leo X, pope of the Catholic Church
